question: What are the two primary materials used to construct a Xylotian 'Sky-Skiff' hull?

answers.ground_truth: The two primary materials used to construct a Xylotian 'Sky-Skiff' hull are 'Aero-Coral' for the frame and 'Noctilucent Metal' for the cladding.
answers.original: The two primary materials used to construct a Xylotian Sky-Skiff hull are hardened Aero-Coral and Noctilucent Metal.

ops.description: Stone-Shells retract into a mineralized shell to protect themselves from threats.

entities.name: Xylotian Sky-Skiff
entities.description: The Xylotian Sky-Skiff is a lightweight aerial vehicle primarily made from hardened Aero-Coral and clad in Noctilucent Metal.

entities.name: Aero-Coral
entities.description: Aero-Coral is a bio-engineered substance used in the construction of Xylotian Sky-Skiffs, grown in Xylos's upper atmosphere.

entities.name: Xylotian Ground Vehicles
entities.description: Xylotian ground vehicles are constructed using volcanic rock for durability.

entities.name: Noctilucent Metal
entities.description: Noctilucent Metal is a thin sheet material used to clad the Aero-Coral frame of Sky-Skiffs, known for glowing faintly in the dark.

relations.description: The Xylotian Sky-Skiff is primarily made from hardened Aero-Coral for its lightweight frame.

The Aero-Coral frame of a Sky-Skiff is clad in thin sheets of Noctilucent Metal for durability and energy shielding.<SEP>Noctilucent Metal glows faintly in the dark, a common aesthetic in Xylotian design.
Training to pilot a Sky-Skiff begins at a young age for many Xylotians.<SEP>Sky-Skiffs are typically piloted by a single Xylotian navigator.
The propulsion system of a Sky-Skiff utilizes focused solar winds.
The Great Xylotian Sky Race features heavily modified Sky-Skiffs.
Navigational tools on a Sky-Skiff include a Star-Compass.
Navigational tools on a Sky-Skiff include a Wind-Gauge.
Xylotian ground vehicles are often made from volcanic rock.

entities.name: Lumina Star
entities.description: Lumina Star is a stable yellow dwarf that provides consistent energy output, supporting diverse environments within the Lumina System.
